OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"As a carpenter in Kansas City juggling family life, rock climbing, baking, and videogames—while keeping my sanity amid the ocean of online noise—I’ve wrestled firsthand with the specter of misinformation. Being married with three kids, my digital footprint is cautious; spending roughly an hour daily on social media isn’t wasted time but deliberate—scouring platforms like Reddit’s r/ChangeMe or following fact-checking accounts to arm myself against false narratives. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a carpenter in Kansas City juggling family life, rock climbing, baking, and videogames—while keeping my sanity amid the ocean of online noise—I’ve wrestled firsthand with the specter of misinformation. My parents’ academic backgrounds (both holding bachelor’s degrees) instilled in me a respect for well-founded knowledge, yet growing up in post-secondary education primary school left gaps I’d later fill through hobbies and self-education. Being married with three kids, my digital footprint is cautious; spending roughly an hour daily on social media isn’t wasted time but deliberate—scouring platforms like Reddit’s r/ChangeMe or following fact-checking accounts to arm myself against false narratives. My online behavior reflects a high openness (immersed in diverse interests) and extraversion (engaging with communities), balanced by moderate conscientiousness and agreeableness, underpinned by low neuroticism—I don’t let things rattle me unduly.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To address fake content management: 1. Leverage trusted fact-checkers and niche expertise like in-person community wisdom paralleling my carpentry precision. 2. Curate feeds to mirror personal interests (rock climbing, baking) ensuring engaging yet credible sources—akin to selecting quality timber. 3. Engage critically as one would inspect materials before crafting—a meticulous eye wards off flaws. 4. Utilize algorithms’ suggestive tendencies by consciously following verified experts fostering extraverted learning akin to community gatherings. This parallels my offline hobbies: structured and social rock climbing groups, recipe exchanges in baking forums; videogame strategy discussions paralleling tactical carpentry planning. Ultimately, blending skepticism with curiosity crafts a resilient defense against misinformation.
